name: Chunkr
tagline: Open-source document intelligence API for layout analysis, OCR, and semantic chunking
seoTitle: Chunkr - Open Source Document Parsing for RAG
metaDescription: Chunkr is an open-source document intelligence API that runs layout analysis, OCR, and semantic chunking to turn PDFs, Office files, and images into RAG-ready chunks.
overview: Chunkr is a self-hosted document intelligence service that converts PDFs, PowerPoint files, Word documents, and images into structured, LLM-ready chunks. It runs layout analysis and OCR with bounding boxes, then outputs structured HTML and Markdown that you can feed into a retrieval pipeline.

It is built for developers who need a document ingestion step in front of a RAG system and want to run it on their own infrastructure. The open-source AGPL release uses community and open-source models, while the separate Chunkr Cloud API runs proprietary in-house models for higher accuracy and adds features like native Excel parsing.

Within the parsing and ingestion category, Chunkr handles the messy first stage of a retrieval pipeline: detecting document structure, reading text from scanned pages, and splitting content into coherent chunks before embedding and indexing.
features: Layout analysis that detects document structure across PDFs, PPTs, Word docs, and images
OCR with bounding boxes so extracted text keeps its position on the page
Outputs structured HTML and Markdown plus semantic chunks ready for RAG and LLM use
Vision-language model processing for handling complex page content
Configurable LLM backends via a models.yaml file or environment variables, using any OpenAI-compatible endpoint
Self-hosted deployment with Docker Compose, with GPU, CPU-only, and Mac ARM compose files

gettingStarted.intro: The open-source version runs locally with Docker Compose. Clone the repo, set up your configuration, and start the services.

steps.heading: Clone the repository
steps.body: You need Docker and Docker Compose installed. The NVIDIA Container Toolkit is optional and only needed for GPU support.

steps.heading: Set up environment and model config
steps.body: Copy the example environment file and the example models file, then edit models.yaml to point at your LLM provider.

steps.heading: Start the services
steps.body: Use the default command for GPU, or pick the CPU-only or Mac ARM compose overrides if you do not have a GPU.
steps.code:
# For GPU deployment:
docker compose up -d

# For CPU-only deployment:
docker compose -f compose.yaml -f compose.cpu.yaml up -d

# For Mac ARM architecture (M1, M2, M3, etc.):
docker compose -f compose.yaml -f compose.cpu.yaml -f compose.mac.yaml up -d

steps.heading: Access the web UI and API
steps.body: Once the containers are running, open the web UI in your browser and send documents to the API.

useCases: Building the ingestion stage of a RAG pipeline that turns PDFs and Office files into clean, chunked text
Extracting text and layout from scanned documents and images where positional bounding boxes matter
Converting reports, slide decks, and Word documents into structured Markdown or HTML for downstream LLM processing
Self-hosting document parsing on your own infrastructure when data cannot leave your environment
